digital_assets.name: PMO Cabinet Page
digital_assets.description: Official complete list of Cabinet appointments

key_people.name: 黄循财 (Lawrence Wong)
key_people.title: Prime Minister, Minister for Finance
key_people.title_description: Head of government; oversees all Cabinet decisions and national fiscal policy
key_people.description: Appointed PM on 15 May 2024. Also serves as Minister for Finance since 15 May 2021. Secretary-General of PAP.

key_people.name: 颜金勇 (Gan Kim Yong)
key_people.title: Deputy Prime Minister, Minister for Trade and Industry
key_people.title_description: Deputy head of government; oversees trade and industry portfolio; Chairman of MAS
key_people.description: Appointed DPM on 15 May 2024. Previously Minister for Health (2011-2021). Co-chaired COVID-19 Multi-Ministry Taskforce.

key_people.name: 李显龙 (Lee Hsien Loong)
key_people.title: Senior Minister
key_people.title_description: Senior advisor in Cabinet; former Prime Minister (2004-2024)
key_people.description: Served as PM from August 2004 to May 2024. Now serves as Senior Minister in the Cabinet.

key_people.name: 尚穆根 (K. Shanmugam)
key_people.title: Coordinating Minister for National Security, Minister for Home Affairs
key_people.title_description: Oversees national security across ministries; heads Home Affairs ministry
key_people.description: Minister for Home Affairs since 1 Oct 2015. Appointed Coordinating Minister for National Security on 23 May 2025.

key_people.name: 陈振声 (Chan Chun Sing)
key_people.title: Coordinating Minister for Public Services, Minister for Defence
key_people.title_description: Coordinates public service policies; oversees defence portfolio
key_people.description: Appointed Coordinating Minister for Public Services and Minister for Defence on 23 May 2025. Previously Minister for Education.

key_people.name: 王乙康 (Ong Ye Kung)
key_people.title: Coordinating Minister for Social Policies, Minister for Health
key_people.title_description: Coordinates social policies across ministries; oversees healthcare
key_people.description: Appointed Coordinating Minister for Social Policies on 23 May 2025. Minister for Health since 15 May 2021.

key_people.name: 维文 (Vivian Balakrishnan)
key_people.title: Minister for Foreign Affairs
key_people.title_description: Heads Singapore's foreign policy and diplomatic relations
key_people.description: Minister for Foreign Affairs since 1 Oct 2015. Key figure in APEC and ASEAN diplomacy.

key_people.name: 傅海燕 (Grace Fu)
key_people.title: Minister for Sustainability and the Environment, Minister-in-charge of Trade Relations
key_people.title_description: Oversees environmental policy and sustainability; manages trade relations
key_people.description: Minister for Sustainability and the Environment since 27 Jul 2020. Minister-in-charge of Trade Relations since 18 Jan 2024.

key_people.name: 杨莉明 (Josephine Teo)
key_people.title: Minister for Digital Development and Information, Minister-in-Charge of Smart Nation and Cybersecurity
key_people.title_description: Oversees digital policy, information, Smart Nation strategy, and cybersecurity
key_people.description: Minister for Digital Development and Information since 2024. Formerly Minister for Manpower (2018-2021) and Minister for Communications and Information (2021-2024).

key_people.name: 马善高 (Masagos Zulkifli)
key_people.title: Minister for Social and Family Development
key_people.title_description: Oversees social welfare, family policy, and community development
key_people.description: Minister for Social and Family Development since 27 Jul 2020.

key_people.name: 李智陞 (Desmond Lee)
key_people.title: Minister for Education, Minister-in-charge of Social Service Integration
key_people.title_description: Oversees national education system and social service coordination
key_people.description: Appointed Minister for Education on 23 May 2025. Previously Minister for National Development.

key_people.name: 英兰妮 (Indranee Rajah)
key_people.title: Minister in the Prime Minister's Office, Second Minister for Finance, Second Minister for National Development
key_people.title_description: Minister in PMO with dual second-minister roles
key_people.description: Minister in PMO since 1 May 2018. Second Minister for Finance and National Development.

key_people.name: 唐振辉 (Edwin Tong)
key_people.title: Minister for Law, Second Minister for Home Affairs
key_people.title_description: Oversees legal affairs; assists in Home Affairs portfolio
key_people.description: Appointed full Minister for Law and Second Minister for Home Affairs on 23 May 2025.

key_people.name: 陈诗龙 (Tan See Leng)
key_people.title: Minister for Manpower, Minister-in-charge of Energy and Science & Technology
key_people.title_description: Oversees workforce and labor policy; energy policy and science & technology
key_people.description: Minister for Manpower since 15 May 2021. Appointed Minister-in-charge of Energy and Science & Technology on 23 May 2025.

key_people.name: 徐芳达 (Chee Hong Tat)
key_people.title: Minister for National Development
key_people.title_description: Oversees urban planning, housing, and national development
key_people.description: Appointed Minister for National Development on 23 May 2025. Previously Minister for Transport.

key_people.name: 梁振伟 (David Neo)
key_people.title: Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth, Senior Minister of State for Education
key_people.title_description: Oversees culture, community, and youth affairs; assists in education
key_people.description: Appointed Acting Minister on 23 May 2025. Former Chief of Army (SAF). First-term MP for Tampines GRC.

key_people.name: 萧振祥 (Jeffrey Siow)
key_people.title: Acting Minister for Transport, Senior Minister of State for Finance
key_people.title_description: Oversees transport policy; assists in finance portfolio
key_people.description: Appointed Acting Minister on 23 May 2025. Former Permanent Secretary (MTI/MOM). First-term MP for Chua Chu Kang GRC.

key_people.name: 费绍尔 (Muhammad Faishal Ibrahim)
key_people.title: Acting Minister-in-Charge of Muslim Affairs, Senior Minister of State for Home Affairs
key_people.title_description: Oversees Muslim community affairs; assists in Home Affairs
key_people.description: Promoted to Senior Minister of State and Acting Minister-in-Charge of Muslim Affairs on 23 May 2025.

departments.name: Prime Minister's Office (PMO)
departments.head: 黄循财 (Lawrence Wong)
departments.description: Supports the Prime Minister in overall governance and coordination of the Cabinet

departments.name: Strategy Group
departments.head: 颜金勇 (Gan Kim Yong)
departments.description: Coordinates whole-of-government strategic priorities; oversees National Climate Change Secretariat and National Population and Talent Division

recent_events.title: Second Lawrence Wong Cabinet announced
recent_events.description: PM Lawrence Wong announced the new Cabinet following the 2025 General Election. Three new Coordinating Ministers appointed. Six of 15 ministries received new ministers. Two first-term MPs (David Neo, Jeffrey Siow) appointed as Acting Ministers.
recent_events.impact: Major Cabinet reshuffle with three new coordinating minister roles created

recent_events.title: 19th Cabinet sworn in
recent_events.description: The Second Lawrence Wong Cabinet was officially sworn in at the Istana. Gan Kim Yong continues as sole DPM.
recent_events.impact: New Cabinet takes effect; 18 full and acting ministers appointed

recent_events.title: GE2025: PAP wins supermajority
recent_events.description: PAP won 87 out of 99 seats in the 2025 General Election, securing Lawrence Wong's mandate to form the new government.
recent_events.impact: PAP supermajority enables PM Wong to form new Cabinet with broad mandate

recent_events.title: Acting Minister David Neo controversy
recent_events.description: Acting Minister David Neo apologised for calling Hong Kong football players and fans 'idiots' during a live-streamed post-match celebration.
recent_events.impact: Minor diplomatic incident; Neo apologised to Hong Kong government

related_entities.org_description: The executive branch of the Government of Singapore, comprising the President and the Cabinet
The legislature; the Cabinet is collectively responsible to Parliament
All current Cabinet members are PAP members; the ruling party since 1959

core_business:
新加坡内阁（Cabinet of Singapore）是新加坡政府的行政决策核心，与总统共同构成行政分支。内阁由总理（Prime Minister）领导，对国会集体负责，拥有政府的总体方向和管控权。内阁决定立法议程，制定各领域的国家政策，并统筹全政府的战略方针。新加坡实行威斯敏斯特议会制，内阁成员由总统根据总理建议从国会议员中任命。

新加坡内阁制度可追溯至1955年的部长会议（Council of Ministers）。1959年新加坡实现完全内部自治后，首届正式内阁由李光耀（Lee Kuan Yew）领导的人民行动党（PAP）组建，PAP自此持续执政。现任为第19届内阁，即第二届黄循财内阁（Second Lawrence Wong Cabinet），于2025年5月23日在总统府（Istana）宣誓就职。总理黄循财同时兼任财政部长；副总理颜金勇（Gan Kim Yong）兼任贸易与工业部长及新加坡金融管理局（MAS）主席。

内阁下设策略组（Strategy Group），由副总理颜金勇领导，负责协调全政府的战略优先事项，涵盖国家气候变化秘书处（National Climate Change Secretariat）及国家人口与人才署（National Population and Talent Division）。2025年5月内阁改组中，新设了三名统筹部长（Coordinating Minister）职位，分别负责国家安全、公共服务和社会政策。两位首任议员——梁振伟（David Neo）和萧振祥（Jeffrey Siow）被任命为代理部长，体现了新一代领导层的培养。

当前内阁优先议题包括经济转型、数字发展、可持续发展、社会凝聚力，以及维持新加坡在APEC、ASEAN等区域和国际事务中的积极参与。新加坡一贯主张以规则为基础的多边贸易体系和开放的区域主义。
apec_stance: Singapore's Cabinet actively participates in APEC through the Prime Minister and relevant ministers (Foreign Affairs, Trade and Industry). Singapore promotes free trade, digital economy cooperation, sustainable development, and regional economic integration within the APEC framework. DPM Gan Kim Yong welcomed China's APEC Chairmanship in 2026. Singapore has consistently advocated for rules-based multilateral trading systems and open regionalism.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with Wikipedia full text + DBpedia (score: 100)
